name: Thích Quảng Đức
cause: Persecution of Buddhists by the Catholic Ngô Đình Diệm regime
broader_context: Buddhist crisis in South Vietnam
aftermath: Intensified international pressure on Diệm regime. Contributed to the coup that overthrew Diệm five months later. The photograph became one of the most iconic images of the 20th century.

name: Thích Nữ Thanh Quang
cause: Protest against the persecution of Buddhists by the Ngô Đình Diệm government
broader_context: Buddhist crisis in South Vietnam (1963)
target: South Vietnamese government / Ngô Đình Diệm regime
aftermath: Part of a wave of self-immolations that contributed to the fall of the Diệm regime in November 1963

name: Thích Nữ Diệu Ngọc
cause: Protest against the persecution of Buddhists by the Diệm government
broader_context: Buddhist crisis in South Vietnam (1963)
target: South Vietnamese government / Ngô Đình Diệm regime
aftermath: Part of the wave of Buddhist self-immolations in 1963 that led to international condemnation of the Diệm regime

name: Alice Herz
cause: Protest against the Vietnam War
broader_context: American anti-Vietnam War movement / early U.S. escalation in Vietnam
aftermath: Died ten days later. She was a German-born Jewish refugee. Considered the first American self-immolation protesting the Vietnam War.

name: Norman Morrison
location_detail: Arlington, Virginia, below Robert McNamara's Pentagon office window
cause: Protest against the Vietnam War and U.S. military policy
broader_context: American anti-Vietnam War movement
target: U.S. government / Secretary of Defense Robert McNamara
aftermath: Largely forgotten in the US but celebrated in North Vietnam, where streets and poems were named after him. He brought his infant daughter Emily with him but handed her to a bystander before igniting.

name: Roger Allen LaPorte
location_detail: New York City, in front of the United Nations
cause: Protest against the Vietnam War
broader_context: American anti-Vietnam War movement
target: U.S. government and the Vietnam War
aftermath: Died the following day. His act came one week after Norman Morrison's self-immolation.

name: Ryszard Siwiec
cause: Protest against the Soviet-led invasion of Czechoslovakia
broader_context: Warsaw Pact invasion of Czechoslovakia / Prague Spring suppression
target: Soviet Union and Warsaw Pact governments
aftermath: His act was suppressed by communist authorities and remained largely unknown until after the fall of communism. He died four days later.

name: Jan Palach
cause: Protest against Soviet occupation and public apathy following the invasion
broader_context: Warsaw Pact invasion of Czechoslovakia, suppression of Prague Spring reforms
aftermath: Sparked widespread mourning and protests. Became a symbol of Czech resistance. His grave became a pilgrimage site. Inspired further self-immolations by Jan Zajíc and Evžen Plocek in the following weeks.

name: Jan Zajíc
cause: Protest against the Soviet-led invasion of Czechoslovakia, following Jan Palach's example
broader_context: Warsaw Pact invasion of Czechoslovakia / normalization period
aftermath: Second in a series of self-immolations in Czechoslovakia following Jan Palach. Less well-known than Palach but honored after 1989.

name: Evžen Plocek
cause: Protest against the Soviet occupation of Czechoslovakia
broader_context: Warsaw Pact invasion of Czechoslovakia / normalization period
aftermath: Third major self-immolation in Czechoslovakia in 1969. Largely suppressed by authorities.

name: Oskar Brüsewitz
cause: Protest against the repression of the church and youth in East Germany
broader_context: Religious persecution and political repression in the GDR
aftermath: Died on August 22. His act drew significant Western media attention and embarrassed the East German government. Became a symbol for the East German opposition movement.

name: Hartmut Gründler
cause: Protest against the West German government's nuclear energy policy
broader_context: Anti-nuclear movement in West Germany
target: West German government / Chancellor Helmut Schmidt
aftermath: Self-immolated during a conference of the Social Democratic Party. His death was largely suppressed in media coverage at the time.

name: Liviu Cornel Babeș
cause: Protest against the Ceaușescu communist regime
broader_context: Repression under Ceaușescu's Romania / lead-up to the 1989 Romanian Revolution
aftermath: His act foreshadowed the Romanian Revolution later that year. Largely suppressed by Romanian media at the time but reported internationally via Radio Free Europe.

name: Homa Darabi
cause: Protest against compulsory hijab and the oppression of women under the Islamic Republic
broader_context: Repression of women's rights in post-revolutionary Iran
aftermath: Her sister Parvin Darabi wrote a book about her life. Became a symbol for Iranian women's rights activism abroad.

name: Malachi Ritscher
cause: Protest against the Iraq War and the Bush administration
broader_context: American anti-Iraq War movement
aftermath: Left a detailed suicide note and mission statement online. Received limited media attention initially; later covered more widely.

name: Tapey
cause: Protest against Chinese rule in Tibet
broader_context: Tibetan self-immolation protests against Chinese occupation
aftermath: Shot by Chinese security forces while burning. Survived but severely injured. Considered the first in the modern wave of Tibetan self-immolations.

name: Mohamed Bouazizi
cause: Humiliation and harassment by municipal officials, confiscation of his cart, lack of economic opportunity
broader_context: Corruption, unemployment, and authoritarian rule in Ben Ali's Tunisia
aftermath: Triggered the Tunisian Revolution, which overthrew Ben Ali within a month. Widely credited as the spark that ignited the Arab Spring across the Middle East and North Africa.

name: Fadwa Laroui
cause: Protest against poor living conditions and denial of public housing
broader_context: Arab Spring wave of self-immolations inspired by Mohamed Bouazizi
aftermath: Her death contributed to the February 20 Movement protests in Morocco during the Arab Spring.

name: Phuntsok
cause: Protest against Chinese rule in Tibet, on the anniversary of 2008 protests
broader_context: Tibetan self-immolation protests
aftermath: His death sparked protests in Ngaba and a heavy security crackdown. Triggered an escalating wave of Tibetan self-immolations through 2012.

name: Palden Choetso
cause: Protest against Chinese rule in Tibet, called for the return of the Dalai Lama
broader_context: Tibetan self-immolation protests
aftermath: Video of her self-immolation was smuggled out and circulated widely. One of the first Tibetan nuns to self-immolate.

name: Jamphel Yeshi
cause: Protest against Chinese President Hu Jintao's visit to India and Chinese rule in Tibet
broader_context: Tibetan self-immolation protests / Hu Jintao's state visit to India
aftermath: Dramatic photographs of his burning body were widely circulated internationally. Died two days later in hospital.

name: Seng-Gye
cause: Protest against Chinese rule in Tibet
broader_context: Tibetan self-immolation protests (2012 peak period)
aftermath: Part of the peak wave of Tibetan self-immolations in 2012, when over 80 such acts occurred.

name: Charles Moore
cause: Protest against racism and racial injustice in America
broader_context: Ongoing racial tensions in the United States
target: Racism in American society, specifically in his hometown
aftermath: Left a note decrying racism. A documentary 'Man on Fire' was made about him. His hometown later erected a monument against racism.

name: Irina Slavina
location_detail: Nizhny Novgorod, in front of the regional Interior Ministry building
cause: Protest against police harassment, searches of her home, and pressure on independent media
broader_context: Crackdown on independent media and civil liberties in Putin-era Russia
aftermath: Left a note saying 'Blame the Russian Federation for my death.' Sparked protests in Nizhny Novgorod and international press freedom condemnations.

name: Wynn Bruce
location_detail: Washington, D.C., in front of the U.S. Supreme Court
cause: Protest against government inaction on climate change (on Earth Day)
broader_context: Climate change activism / environmental movement
target: U.S. government / Supreme Court / fossil fuel policy
aftermath: Died the following day. Friends confirmed it was a planned act of protest inspired by Thích Quảng Đức. Received significant but brief media coverage.

name: Aaron Bushnell
cause: Protest against U.S. complicity in the Israeli military campaign in Gaza
broader_context: Israel-Gaza war following October 7, 2023 attack, U.S. military aid to Israel
target: U.S. government, U.S. military support for Israel
aftermath: Reignited debate over U.S. involvement in the Gaza conflict. Became a symbol within the pro-Palestinian movement. Prompted discussions about moral injury within the U.S. military.

name: Mohammad Asif Javed Jutt
cause: Nine-year failed legal battle against Nestlé after being fired for attempting to form a union
broader_context: Labor rights suppression by multinational corporations in Pakistan, inaccessible justice system for working class
aftermath: Left-wing parties and trade unions formed an alliance to seek justice and support his family. Sparked renewed debate about labor rights and corporate impunity in Pakistan.
